source.title: Lateral habenula as a source of negative reward signals in dopamine neurons

confidence_rationale: Direct primate single-unit demonstration that LHb neurons fire to no-reward cues and that LHb stimulation drives DA inhibition with the latency relationship necessary for LHb to be upstream of the dopamine dip. As a candidate broadcast invalidation trigger, this is the strongest substrate-existence paper in the pull: LHb broadcasts a 'this is worse than predicted' signal via the rostromedial tegmental nucleus to VTA/SNc. Mapping fidelity to V_s remains moderate because the signal is reward-PE-shaped, not model-fit-shaped.
failure_signatures: LHb hyperactivity in learned helplessness (later literature) suggests *over*-invalidation of agency-anchors as a failure mode -- the broadcast trigger fires too easily, every anchor is downweighted, and the agent stops attempting any structured behaviour. Architecturally relevant: this is the symmetric failure to MECH-284's accumulator-never-trips phenotype.

mapping.source_claim_statement: LHb neurons in macaque are excited by no-reward-predicting cues and inhibited by reward-predicting cues; weak LHb electrical stimulation produces strong inhibition of midbrain DA neurons; LHb excitation precedes DA inhibition on unrewarded trials.
mapping.ree_translation: LHb is a candidate substrate for a broadcast invalidation trigger upstream of the DA dip in MECH-284. The signal it carries -- 'predicted reward absent' -- is the right computational shape for the V_s downweight trigger but the wrong content (reward-PE rather than schema-fit-PE). The architectural reading would be: LHb generates the broadcast event; downstream DA dip distributes it; MECH-284 accumulator integrates dips per schema; MECH-269 reset criterion fires when the integral exceeds a per-schema threshold.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The signal in Matsumoto2007 is specifically reward-omission. Whether LHb also fires on schema-fit violations independent of reward is not established by this paper; that extension comes from Bromberg-Martin & Hikosaka 2011 (information-PE) and is the architectural bridge to V_s. The clinical mapping (LHb-hyperactive = depression / learned helplessness) is suggestive but not in this paper.
mapping.source_context: Macaque single-unit LHb and SNc recordings during a visually guided saccade task with positionally biased reward outcomes; LHb micro-stimulation experiment establishing causal direction.

confidence_components.notes: Nature primate single-unit + causal stimulation. Mapping moderate because reward-PE -> schema-fit-PE extension still required. Transfer low because LHb circuitry is conserved primate-to-human and the clinical correlate is robustly replicated.
